items.title: Type 1 Diabetes and Incident Dementia: An Analysis in the All of Us Cohort.
items.abstract: Although diabetes mellitus (DM) is a well-established determinant of dementia risk, most studies have evaluated type 2 DM (T2DM) or any DM. The influence of type 1 DM (T1DM) on dementia risk remains unclear. We evaluated associations of T1DM and T2DM, separately, with incident dementia using linked electronic health records (EHRs). This prospective cohort study used previously collected survey and EHR data from the All of Us (AoU) cohort, a convenience sample of US adults. Eligible participants were 50 years or older and completed baseline surveys. Enrollment began in 2017, with data available through October 2023, along with records before enrollment. Mean follow-up from baseline was 2.4 years. We developed an algorithm to distinguish DM type based on count of T1DM encounters. This algorithm was validated against 2 reference measures: self-reported diabetes type and C-peptide values. Using AoU data, we classified participants as having no DM, T1DM, or T2DM. We ascertained incident dementia using ICD-9, ICD-10, and Systematized Nomenclature of Medicine codes in participants' EHRs. We estimated hazard ratios (HRs) and 95% CIs for the association of diabetes type with incident dementia using Cox proportional hazards models. Among 283,772 participants (mean [SD] age 64.62 [8.96] years; 56.7% women), 60.3% identified as non-Hispanic White and 13.3% as Hispanic/Latino. Optimal DM classification algorithm cutoffs varied by reference standard: self-reported diabetes: ≥1 T1DM EHR encounter (sensitivity: 0.59; specificity: 0.90) and C-peptide: ≥3 T1DM EHR encounters (sensitivity: 0.76; specificity: 0.79). Defining T1DM as having ≥1 T1DM encounter, 5,442 participants had T1DM. Compared with those without DM, participants with T1DM had higher dementia incidence (sociodemographic-adjusted HR 2.82; 95% CI 2.28-3.48) and those with T2DM also had elevated risk (sociodemographic-adjusted HR 2.08; 95% CI 2.87-2.31). Results were similar across sex, race, and ethnicity strata. In AoU, DM was associated with elevated dementia risk, with the highest risk among those with T1DM. These findings highlight the need to better understand mechanisms tying T1DM to dementia.
items.summary_ja:
背景：糖尿病（DM）は認知症リスクの既知の因子であるが、これまでの研究は主に2型糖尿病（T2DM）や糖尿病全般を対象としており、1型糖尿病（T1DM）と認知症リスクの関連は不明であった。  
方法：米国成人を対象としたAll of Us（AoU）コホートの電子カルテ（EHR）と調査データを用い、50歳以上の参加者約28万人を平均2.4年間追跡。T1DMとT2DMを区別するアルゴリズムを開発・検証し、ICDコードにより新規認知症発症を判定。コックス比例ハザードモデルで糖尿病タイプ別の認知症リスクを評価した。  
結果：T1DM患者5,442人は、糖尿病なし群と比較し認知症発症リスクが有意に高く（調整後ハザード比2.82、95%CI 2.28-3.48）、T2DM患者もリスク上昇が認められた（調整後HR 2.08、95%CI 2.87-2.31）。性別、人種、民族別でも同様の傾向を示した。  
結論：T1DMは認知症リスクを特に高めることが示され、T1DMと認知症の関連メカニズムの解明が重要である。
items.summary_short_ja: 本研究は米国のAll of Usコホートを用い、1型糖尿病（T1DM）と2型糖尿病（T2DM）が認知症発症リスクに与える影響を検討した。50歳以上の約28万人を対象に電子カルテ情報から糖尿病タイプを分類し、認知症発症を追跡。T1DM患者は非糖尿病者に比べ認知症リスクが約2.8倍、T2DM患者も約2.1倍高かった。性別・人種を問わず同様の傾向が認められ、T1DMと認知症の関連解明の重要性が示された。

items.title: Frequency of AQP4 and MOG Antibodies in Patients With Optic Neuritis Fulfilling Minimal New Multiple Sclerosis MRI Criteria.
items.abstract: Recent revisions to multiple sclerosis (MS) diagnostic criteria include the optic nerve as a site of dissemination in space, enabling this diagnosis in patients with acute optic neuritis (ON) and a single additional MS-typical location on MRI if dissemination in time (DIT) is demonstrated. We aimed to assess the frequency of non-MS diagnoses in this context. We retrospectively analyzed consecutive patients with inaugural acute ON and at least 1 MS-typical lesion in a single brain location on baseline MRI across 3 French centers. All patients met DIT criteria and underwent aquaporin-4 (AQP4) and myelin oligodendrocyte glycoprotein (MOG) antibody testing. Final diagnoses were based on clinical, radiologic, and follow-up data. Among 96 patients (mean age 35.8 years; 70.8% female), 73 (76.0%) were diagnosed with MS and 23 (24.0%) with MOG antibody-associated disease (n = 18) or neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder. Longitudinally extensive lesions, bilateral involvement, chiasmal enhancement, or optic perineuritis were observed in 100% of non-MS patients and 24.6% of patients with MS (difference 75.4%, 95% CI: 64.3-86.6;  AQP4 and MOG antibody testing, along with careful orbital MRI interpretation, is recommended in patients with ON meeting minimal new MS criteria.
items.summary_ja:
背景：多発性硬化症（MS）の診断基準改訂により、視神経が病変の空間的拡散部位に含まれ、急性視神経炎（ON）患者でMRIにMS典型病変が1か所あれば時間的拡散（DIT）を示すことでMS診断が可能となった。しかし、この基準下での非MS疾患の頻度は不明であった。  
方法：フランスの3施設で、初発急性ON患者96名（平均年齢35.8歳、女性70.8％）を対象に、ベースラインMRIでMS典型病変が1か所以上ある患者を後ろ向きに解析。全員がDIT基準を満たし、AQP4抗体およびMOG抗体検査を実施。最終診断は臨床、画像、経過観察に基づく。  
結果：96例中73例（76.0％）がMS、23例（24.0％）がMOG抗体関連疾患（18例）または視神経脊髄炎スペクトラム障害と診断された。非MS群は長大病変、両側性病変、視交叉の造影効果、視神経周囲炎を100％認めたのに対し、MS群は24.6％であった（差75.4％、95％CI:64.3-86.6）。  
結論：新しいMS診断基準を満たすON患者では、AQP4およびMOG抗体検査と詳細な眼窩MRI評価が推奨される。
items.summary_short_ja: 急性視神経炎（ON）で新しい多発性硬化症（MS）MRI基準を満たす患者96例を解析した結果、76％がMS、24％がMOG抗体関連疾患または視神経脊髄炎スペクトラム障害と診断された。非MS患者は長大病変や両側性病変、視交叉の造影効果などを示し、AQP4およびMOG抗体検査と詳細な眼窩MRI評価が重要とされた。

items.title: CTA-Based Plaque-RADS to Assess Etiologic Role of Nonstenotic Carotid Plaques in Patients With Embolic Stroke of Undetermined Source.
items.abstract: Nonstenotic (<50%) carotid plaques (NSCPs) are increasingly recognized as potential embolic sources in embolic stroke of undetermined source (ESUS), but their etiologic role remains uncertain because of limited data and the lack of standardized definition of high-risk plaque. We evaluated the clinical applicability and reliability of a modified CT angiography (CTA)-based Plaque-Reporting and Data System (RADS) classification for characterizing NSCPs in ESUS and examined the association between high-risk NSCPs and stroke laterality, the coexistence of competing stroke mechanisms, and the risk of stroke recurrence. We conducted a single-center cohort study (January 2016-June 2025) including consecutive ESUS patients with unilateral anterior circulation stroke and neck CTA, retrospectively and prospectively enrolled. NSCPs were classified using a modified CTA-based Plaque-RADS: (1) normal vessel wall; (2) maximum wall thickness (MWT) <3 mm; (3) MWT ≥3 mm (3ab) or ulceration (3c); (4) intraplaque hemorrhage (4a) or intraluminal thrombus (4c). Patients were stratified according to the presence of ipsilateral high-risk Plaque-RADS (categories 3-4) vs ipsilateral low-risk Plaque-RADS (categories 1-2). Inter-rater agreement for CTA-based Plaque-RADS was assessed, and associations between high-risk Plaque-RADS and stroke side, competing embolic sources, and recurrent ischemic events were evaluated with multivariable regression and survival analyses. Among 512 patients (median age 70 years, 47.8% women), 109 (21.3%) had ipsilateral high-risk and 403 (78.7%) had low-risk Plaque-RADS scores. Inter-rater agreement was excellent (Cohen κ = 0.83,  CTA-based Plaque-RADS can be reliably applied in routine clinical practice to categorize NSCPs in ESUS. Ipsilateral high-risk NSCPs are strongly associated with the index stroke side, lower prevalence of competing cardioembolic sources, and increased ipsilateral recurrence, supporting their role as a distinct causal mechanism within ESUS and underscoring the need for randomized trials on secondary prevention.
items.summary_ja:
背景：非狭窄性（50％未満）頸動脈プラーク（NSCP）が原因不明の塞栓性脳卒中（ESUS）における塞栓源として注目されているが、その病因的役割は不明確であり、高リスクプラークの標準的定義も確立していない。  
方法：単一施設で2016年1月から2025年6月までに前方循環の片側ESUS患者512例を対象に、頸部CTAを用いた修正版Plaque-RADS分類（カテゴリー1～4）でNSCPを評価し、評価者間一致度や高リスクプラーク（カテゴリー3-4）と脳卒中側、他の塞栓源の共存、再発リスクとの関連を多変量解析および生存分析で検討した。  
結果：患者の21.3％が片側に高リスクプラークを有し、評価者間一致度は極めて高かった（κ=0.83）。高リスクプラークは脳卒中発症側と強く関連し、心原性塞栓源の共存は少なく、同側の再発リスクが増加していた。  
結論：CTAベースのPlaque-RADSは臨床で信頼性高くNSCPを分類可能であり、高リスクNSCPはESUSの独立した病因機序として重要である。二次予防のための無作為化試験の必要性が示唆された。
items.summary_short_ja: 本研究は、非狭窄性頸動脈プラーク（NSCP）が原因不明の塞栓性脳卒中（ESUS）に果たす役割を評価するため、修正CTAベースのPlaque-RADS分類の臨床的有用性と信頼性を検証した。512例のESUS患者を対象に解析し、高リスクNSCPは発症側の脳卒中と強く関連し、競合する塞栓源の頻度が低く、再発リスクも高いことを示した。Plaque-RADSは臨床でのNSCP評価に有効であり、二次予防のための無作為試験の必要性を示唆する。

items.title: Contingency of Plasma Dementia Biomarkers on Cognitive Profiles for Prognosis of Incident Dementia: The ARIC Study.
items.abstract: Plasma biomarkers such as phospho-tau species are increasingly used in clinical practice for the diagnosis of Alzheimer disease (AD). Phosphorylated-tau at threonine 181 (p-tau181) values also provide prognostic information about incident dementia. Cognitive status similarly conveys prognostic information, but the relationship between plasma biomarkers for AD and cognitive status requires clarification. Participants from the Atherosclerosis Risk in Communities (ARIC) study who were adjudicated as free of dementia in 2011-2013 had plasma samples analyzed for p-tau181 and other biomarkers. Participants were surveilled for incident dementia through December 31, 2022. Cumulative incidence curves, Cox models, and Fine-Gray models were used to evaluate the independent and combined discriminatory accuracy of cognitive status and plasma biomarkers for incident dementia. The sample comprised 1,577 ARIC participants (age 76.5 years, 60% women, 73% White, 27% Black). The risk of incident dementia was higher in persons with a baseline status of mild cognitive impairment (covariate-adjusted hazard ratio [HR] 2.94, 95% CI 2.61-3.33) compared with those who were cognitively unimpaired independent of biomarker status. The risk of dementia was also higher in persons with more abnormal concentrations of p-tau181 and other biomarkers independent of cognitive status. When age, cognitive status, and p-tau181 were included in the same models, the risk was attenuated relative to models where only cognitive status or plasma biomarkers were included. For continuous p-tau181 concentrations, the covariate-adjusted HR without cognitive status was 1.45 (95% CI 1.36-1.54), but when cognitive status was included, the HR decreased to 1.37 (95% CI 1.29-1.46). Models showed that when combined with age, p-tau181 alone, cognitive status alone, or the combination of p-tau181 and cognitive status had similar discriminatory accuracy. Cognitive status and plasma biomarker concentrations convey independent but overlapping information about the risk of incident dementia. Although cognitive status and plasma p-tau181 have similar discriminatory accuracies, the far lower incidence rate of dementia in persons who are initially cognitively unimpaired highlights the importance of an accurate clinical diagnosis.
items.summary_ja:
背景：アルツハイマー病（AD）の診断において、血漿中のリン酸化タウ（p-tau181）などのバイオマーカーが注目されているが、認知機能状態との関係は明確でない。  
方法：認知症がないと判定されたARIC研究参加者1,577名（平均年齢76.5歳）を対象に、2011-2013年に採取した血漿中のp-tau181などのバイオマーカーを測定し、2022年まで認知症発症を追跡。認知機能状態とバイオマーカーの独立および併用による認知症予測能を統計モデルで評価した。  
結果：軽度認知障害（MCI）群は認知機能正常群に比べ認知症リスクが約3倍高く（HR 2.94）、p-tau181の異常値も認知機能状態に関係なくリスク上昇に寄与した。年齢、認知機能状態、p-tau181を同時に考慮するとリスク推定はやや低下したが、認知機能状態単独、p-tau181単独、両者併用のいずれも同程度の予測精度を示した。  
結論：認知機能状態と血漿p-tau181は認知症リスクに関して独立かつ重複する情報を提供し、両者は同等の予測能力を持つ。特に認知機能正常者の認知症発症率が低いことから、正確な臨床診断の重要性が示唆された。
items.summary_short_ja: 本研究は、認知機能状態と血漿中のリン酸化タウ（p-tau181）などのバイオマーカーが、認知症発症リスクの予測において独立かつ重複した情報を提供することを示した。認知機能障害や異常なp-tau181濃度はそれぞれ発症リスクを高めるが、両者を組み合わせた予測精度は単独使用と同程度であった。認知機能が正常な場合の発症率が低いことから、正確な臨床診断の重要性が強調される。

items.title: Coffee and Tea Intake, Dementia Risk, and Cognitive Function.
items.abstract: Evidence linking coffee and tea to cognitive health remains inconclusive, and most studies fail to differentiate caffeinated from decaffeinated coffee. To investigate associations of coffee and tea intake with dementia risk and cognitive function. Prospective cohort study that included female participants from the Nurses' Health Study (NHS; n = 86 606 with data from 1980-2023) and male participants from the Health Professionals Follow-up Study (HPFS; n = 45 215 with data from 1986-2023) who did not have cancer, Parkinson disease, or dementia at study entry (baseline) in the US. The primary exposures were intakes of caffeinated coffee, decaffeinated coffee, and tea. Dietary intake was collected every 2 to 4 years using validated food frequency questionnaires. The primary outcome was dementia, which was identified via death records and physician diagnoses. The secondary outcomes included subjective cognitive decline assessed by a questionnaire-based score (range, 0-7; higher scores indicate greater perceived decline; cases defined as those with a score ≥3) and objective cognitive function assessed only in the NHS cohort using telephone-based neuropsychological tests such as the Telephone Interview for Cognitive Status (TICS) score (range, 0-41) and a measure of global cognition (a standardized mean z score for all 6 administered cognitive tests). Among 131 821 participants (mean age at baseline, 46.2 [SD, 7.2] years in the NHS cohort and 53.8 [SD, 9.7] years in the HPFS cohort; 65.7% were female) during up to 43 years of follow-up (median, 36.8 years; IQR, 28-42 years), there were 11 033 cases of incident dementia. After adjusting for potential confounders and pooling results across cohorts, higher caffeinated coffee intake was significantly associated with lower dementia risk (141 vs 330 cases per 100 000 person-years comparing the fourth [highest] quartile of consumption with the first [lowest] quartile; hazard ratio, 0.82 [95% CI, 0.76 to 0.89]) and lower prevalence of subjective cognitive decline (7.8% vs 9.5%, respectively; prevalence ratio, 0.85 [95% CI, 0.78 to 0.93]). In the NHS cohort, higher caffeinated coffee intake was also associated with better objective cognitive performance. Compared with participants in the lowest quartile, those in the highest quartile had a higher mean TICS score (mean difference, 0.11 [95% CI, 0.01 to 0.21]) and a higher mean global cognition score (mean difference, 0.02 [95% CI, -0.01 to 0.04]); however, the association with global cognition was not statistically significant (P = .06). Higher intake of tea showed similar associations with these cognitive outcomes, whereas decaffeinated coffee intake was not associated with lower dementia risk or better cognitive performance. A dose-response analysis showed nonlinear inverse associations of caffeinated coffee and tea intake levels with dementia risk and subjective cognitive decline. The most pronounced associated differences were observed with intake of approximately 2 to 3 cups per day of caffeinated coffee or 1 to 2 cups per day of tea. Greater consumption of caffeinated coffee and tea was associated with lower risk of dementia and modestly better cognitive function, with the most pronounced association at moderate intake levels.
items.summary_ja:
背景：コーヒーやお茶の摂取と認知機能や認知症リスクの関連は明確でなく、多くの研究でカフェイン入りとカフェイン抜きの区別がされていない。  
方法：米国の看護師健康研究（NHS、女性86,606名）と健康専門職追跡研究（HPFS、男性45,215名）を対象に、1980～2023年のデータを用いた前向きコホート研究。カフェイン入りコーヒー、カフェイン抜きコーヒー、お茶の摂取量を2～4年ごとに食事頻度調査で評価し、認知症発症（死亡記録・医師診断）や主観的認知低下、NHSでは電話による認知機能検査も実施。  
結果：131,821名を最大43年追跡し、11,033件の認知症発症を確認。カフェイン入りコーヒーの摂取が多いほど認知症リスクが低く（最高摂取群のハザード比0.82）、主観的認知低下の有病率も低かった。また、NHSでは認知機能スコアも高かった。お茶の摂取も同様の関連を示したが、カフェイン抜きコーヒーは関連なし。摂取量とリスクの関係は非線形で、カフェイン入りコーヒーは1日2～3杯、お茶は1～2杯で最も効果的だった。  
結論：カフェイン入りコーヒーとお茶の適度な摂取は認知症リスクの低下および認知機能の改善と関連し、カフェイン抜きコーヒーにはその効果が認められなかった。
items.summary_short_ja: 本研究は、米国の大規模コホートを対象に、カフェイン入りコーヒー・紅茶の摂取と認知症リスクおよび認知機能の関連を調査した。約43年の追跡で、カフェイン入りコーヒーや紅茶の適度な摂取（コーヒー2～3杯、紅茶1～2杯/日）が認知症リスクの低下と主観的・客観的認知機能の改善と関連した。一方、デカフェコーヒーには有意な効果は認められなかった。
